operating_state: Total sales and revenues decreased 10% to $14.249 billion in Q1 2025, driven by lower sales volume of $1.1 billion and unfavorable price realization of $250 million. Sales declined across all three primary segments, with volume decline primarily driven by dealer inventory changes increasing only $100 million versus $1.4 billion in the prior year. Operating profit decreased to $2.6 billion from $3.5 billion, compressing operating profit margin to 18.1% from 22.3%.
cost_structure: Cost of goods sold improved as a percentage of sales despite lower revenue, declining to $9.0 billion from $9.7 billion. Operating profit margin compressed to 18.1% from 22.3% in the prior year, with adjusted operating profit margin at 18.3% versus 22.2%. Manufacturing costs increased unfavorably, particularly in Energy & Transportation, with the company expecting $250-350 million of tariff-related costs in Q2 2025.
material_events: The company repurchased $3.660 billion of common stock in Q1 2025, reducing basic shares outstanding to approximately 471 million shares. The Board maintained the quarterly dividend at $1.41 per share, with $674 million paid in dividends during the quarter. Order backlog increased approximately $5.0 billion to $35.0 billion at quarter-end. Cash position declined substantially to $3.6 billion from $6.9 billion at year-end 2024, primarily driven by the aggressive share buyback program.
macro_exposures: The company faces significant tariff exposure with $250-350 million of estimated costs in Q2 2025 from announced and implemented tariffs. Currency impacts were unfavorable by $222 million, primarily from Brazilian real, Australian dollar, and euro weakness against the US dollar. Management has evaluated various scenarios due to tariff announcements and increasing economic uncertainty affecting global operations.
forward_outlook: Management expects full-year 2025 sales to be about flat compared to 2024 in a pre-tariff scenario, or down slightly in an alternative scenario assuming negative economic growth in the second half. Capital expenditures are expected to be approximately $2.5 billion for 2025, with restructuring costs anticipated.
uncertainty_profile: The company cannot derive an accurate estimate of the net full-year 2025 impact of tariffs given uncertainty around rates and timing of mitigation actions. Past dues at Cat Financial were 1.58% at quarter-end, with allowance for credit losses at 0.95% of finance receivables. The company maintains covenant compliance with consolidated net worth above required thresholds.
